audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for man born blind, anti-victim-blame opening, clay/washing, Sabbath dispute, parents, expulsion, Son of Humanity, sight/judgment reversal.

verses.restored: Passing by, he saw a human blind from birth.
His disciples questioned him, Rabbi, who sinned/misaligned, this one or his parents, that he was born blind?
Jesus answered, Neither this one sinned/misaligned nor his parents, but so that the works of the Divine might be revealed in him.
It is necessary for us to work the works of the One who sent me while it is day; night comes when no one is able to work.
While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
Saying these things, he spat on the ground, made clay from the saliva, and anointed the man's eyes with the clay.
He said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, which is translated Sent. He went, therefore, washed, and came seeing.
The neighbors and those who had seen him before, that he was a beggar, were saying, Is this not the one sitting and begging?
Others were saying, This is he; others were saying, No, but he is like him. He said, I am he.
They were saying to him, How then were your eyes opened?
He answered, The human called Jesus made clay, anointed my eyes, and said to me, Go to Siloam and wash. So I went, washed, and received sight.
They said to him, Where is that one? He says, I do not know.
They bring to the Pharisees the one formerly blind.
Now it was Sabbath on the day Jesus made the clay and opened his eyes.
Again, therefore, the Pharisees also were asking him how he received sight. He said to them, He put clay on my eyes, and I washed, and I see.

Some of the Pharisees were saying, This human is not from the Divine, because he does not keep the Sabbath. But others were saying, How is a sinful/misaligned human able to do such signs? And there was division among them.
They therefore say to the blind one again, What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes? He said, He is a prophet.
The Judeans therefore did not trust concerning him that he had been blind and received sight until they called the parents of the one who received sight.
They questioned them, saying, Is this your son, whom you say was born blind? How then does he now see?
His parents answered and said, We know that this is our son and that he was born blind.
But how he now sees, we do not know; or who opened his eyes, we do not know. Ask him; he has age. He will speak concerning himself.
His parents said these things because they feared the Judeans, for the Judeans had already agreed that if anyone confessed him as Messiah, that one would be put out of the synagogue.
Because of this his parents said, He has age; ask him.
Therefore they called the human a second time, the one who had been blind, and said to him, Give glory to the Divine; we know that this human is sinful/misaligned.
That one answered, Whether he is sinful/misaligned I do not know. One thing I know: being blind, now I see.
They said to him, What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?
He answered them, I told you already, and you did not hear. Why do you want to hear again? Do you also want to become his disciples?
They insulted him and said, You are that one's disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
We know that the Divine has spoken to Moses; but this one, we do not know where he is from.
The human answered and said to them, In this is a marvel: you do not know where he is from, and he opened my eyes.
We know that the Divine does not hear sinners/misaligned ones, but if anyone is reverent toward the Divine and does his will, this one he hears.
From the age it has not been heard that anyone opened the eyes of one born blind.
If this one were not from the Divine, he would not be able to do anything.
They answered and said to him, You were born entirely in sins/misalignments, and you teach us? And they cast him outside.
Jesus heard that they had cast him outside, and finding him, said, Do you trust into the Son of Humanity?
He answered and said, And who is he, Lord, so that I may trust into him?
Jesus said to him, You have both seen him, and the one speaking with you is that one.
He said, I trust, Lord; and he worshiped him.
Jesus said, For judgment I came into this world, so that those not seeing may see, and those seeing may become blind.
Some of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things and said to him, Are we also blind?
Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you would not have sin/misalignment; but now you say, We see. Your sin/misalignment remains.
